lede: A look at January 23 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Walter Frederick Morrison and Julio Enciso (footballer, born 2004), and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Northwestern Air Flight 738
events.desc: Northwestern Air Flight 738 crashes after takeoff from Fort Smith Airport, Northwest Territories, Canada, killing six people.

events.title: Daniel Pearl
events.desc: U.S. journalist Daniel Pearl is kidnapped in Karachi, Pakistan and subsequently murdered.

events.title: 2001 Tiananmen Square self-immolation incident
events.desc: Five people attempt to set themselves on fire in Beijing's Tiananmen Square, an act that many people later claim is staged by the Chinese Communist Party to frame Falun Gong and thus escalate their persecution.

events.title: Madeleine Albright
events.desc: Madeleine Albright becomes the first woman to serve as United States Secretary of State.

events.title: USS Pueblo (AGER-2)
events.desc: USS Pueblo (AGER-2) is attacked and seized by the Korean People's Navy.

events.title: Walter Frederick Morrison
events.desc: American inventor Walter Frederick Morrison sells the rights to his flying disc to the Wham-O toy company, which later renames it the "Frisbee".

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Battle of Rabaul commences Japan's invasion of Australia's Territory of New Guinea.

events.title: First International Opium Convention
events.desc: The First International Opium Convention is signed at The Hague.

events.title: Elizabeth Blackwell
events.desc: Elizabeth Blackwell is awarded her M.D. by the Geneva Medical College of Geneva, New York, becoming the United States' first female doctor.

events.title: Hongwu Emperor
events.desc: Zhu Yuanzhang proclaims himself the Hongwu Emperor, beginning the Ming dynasty.

deaths.text: Charles Osgood, American radio and television commentator, writer and musician
Robert W. Scott, American farmer and politician, 67th Governor of North Carolina
T. M. Sabaratnam, Sri Lankan lawyer and politician

observances.text: Christian feast day: Espousals of the Blessed Virgin Mary
